MetaInformation.Description: Property details for St. Thomas Street. One of many properties for sale in Lymington, Hampshire, SO41 9NA from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: A landmark Grade II* listed property of great grandeur and history with a bespoke interior of contemporary design, finished to the highest quality and set at the heart of Lymington.
Location: Set in an exclusive and sought-after location on the High Street, within minutes of the deep water marinas, world renowned yachting and sailing facilities and stunning coastal walks along The Solent Way. The Georgian market town of Lymington offers extensive restaurants and boutiques and a picturesque harbour at the bottom of the cobbled streets to the quay. Lymington is surrounded by the outstanding natural
beauty of the New Forest National Park.

There is a branch line train link from Lymington Town station to Brockenhurst Railway Station (approximately 5.5 miles) which provides a half hourly service to London Waterloo with a journey time of approximately 90 minutes.
There is also a range of both private and state schooling, with the popular Walhampton preparatory school close by.

LongDescription.Body:
The spirit of the house is revealed from the hallway flanked by four beautifully appointed reception rooms. As soon as one enters through the arched stained-glass doorway, a frameless glazed wall both separates and reveals the panelled drawing room with mood lighting and exquisitely realistic gas open fire. The adjoining library overlooks the garden again with an exquisite fireplace followed by the spacious cinema room with projector and retractable screen which has as its backdrop an open log fire. Opposite is the breath-taking dining room with views onto the south facing garden. Throughout these rooms, which form the historic core of the house, the interior has been crafted to assimilate contemporary style and convenience in harmony with the original Grade II* Listed features, materials and craftsmanship that make this house unique. Suspended ceilings with hidden and recessed lighting highlight the original painted wall panelling throughout. All original sash windows have been completely refurbished reinstating the box shutters in which to enjoy music or film scores via the hardwired surround sound systems unobtrusively positioned in each room.

The beautiful original 17th Century staircase with elaborately carved newel posts and balustrades leads to the first and second floors from the hall. A second means of descending from the first floor is via a polished stainless steel tube slide which passes through a glass floor, designed for those with a sense of fun and a great talking point to liven up any dinner party! Leading from these exceptional reception rooms is a spacious wing designed around family life. The fully fitted bespoke kitchen has been designed with immaculate lines and discreetly positioned appliances including full height double wine fridges. There is underfloor heating throughout the kitchen which is finished in white Corian and features a copious food preparation surface incorporating a sunken ice trough from which to serve fresh sea food or champagne. The whole blends seamlessly with an informal seating and dining area with expansive floor to ceiling Skyframe sliding doors sourced from Switzerland which open on to the garden to the south and west elevations. There is a media wall with large, recessed television beneath which is a contemporary full width gas fireplace. Opening off this space is a superb games room, again featuring a bespoke television area and ample space for pool and table tennis tables. The practicalities have not been overlooked and a vast laundry room, cloakrooms and shower rooms complete the ground floor accommodation.

There is also a plant room and a lift leading to the first floor. To the first floor, the main bedroom features a full media suite of television and surround sound system, as do all other main bedrooms. The adjoining bathroom reaches an even higher level of luxurious tranquillity with a vast walk-in shower and twin slimline baths angled to face a perfectly positioned television. Separating bedroom and bathroom is a beautifully appointed dressing room. There are four further bedrooms on this floor including two guest suites with both dressing areas and en suite shower rooms. Above the hall leading to the kitchen is the glass floored chill out area visible from the kitchen below. The original staircase rises again to the spacious second floor accommodation where there are four further double bedrooms, two of which incorporate en suite facilities, and a family bathroom.

The ingenuity, imagination, style and sense of fun that has infused every square inch of this historic house is awaiting its next fortunate owners.

OUTSIDE
The house has a beautiful approach through a wrought iron pedestrian gate beneath a floral arch where the pathway leads to the front door, flanked by flower beds and mature planting. There is also a double electric vehicular gate to the side of the house which leads through to an extensive parking area and courtyard, as well as to the impressive tandem garage. Above the garage, and accessed via an external staircase, is a beautifully presented self-contained annexe with an open plan kitchen, sitting room and dining area as well as a double bedroom with built in wardrobes and a beautifully fitted shower room. The south facing garden has been carefully designed to blend seamlessly with the living space. The stone paving that surrounds the garden is precisely level with the internal floor level and when the slimline sliding doors from the kitchen are opened the garden becomes another room and beautiful space from which to admire the glorious rear façade. The majority of the garden is laid to a highly realistic artificial grass requiring little maintenance. There is a discrete space for a hot tub and ample room for relaxing or outdoor dining.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Alumhurst Road. One of many properties for sale in Bournemouth, Dorset, BH4 8EW from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: A charming five-bedroom detached Edwardian home, rich in original features and ideally positioned just moments from the beach.
Location: The property is situated just minutes from the highly popular Alum Chine beach, renowned for its award-winning Blue Flag sandy bathing beaches.

The vibrant shopping village of Westbourne is less than a mile away and offers a wide range of independent shops, boutiques, bars, and restaurants, while Canford Cliffs Village is approximately 1.4 miles away.

Transport links are excellent. Parkstone an Bournemouth mainline railway station provides services to Southampton and London. Bournemouth and Southampton International Airports are also nearby, and Poole Harbour offers ferry services to the Channel Islands and mainland Europe.

Local Beach 0.5 miles, Westbourne shops 0.5 miles, Canford Cliffs Village 1.6 miles , Bournemouth town centre 1.7 miles, Sandbanks (Shore Road) 2.2 miles, Bournemouth Airport 7.7 miles, London 110 miles (2 hours by train). All times and distances are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
This substantial Edwardian detached residence occupies a level plot and offers versatile, well-proportioned accommodation arranged over three floors. Refurbished to a modern standard, the property is beautifully presented throughout, with all rooms being notably generous in size, and it retains many original features, including high ceilings on both the ground and first floors. It is superbly located in the highly desirable Alum Chine area, within an easy sub-10-minute walk of the beach, Westbourne Village and the Chines, and just moments from the award-winning sandy beaches.

The bright and spacious accommodation includes an additional self-contained studio annex, currently used as a successful holiday rental but equally suitable as a family annex, created by the current owners.

Upon entering the property, you are welcomed by an impressive reception hall with stairs rising to the first floor and access to the principal ground floor rooms. The ground floor offers two generous reception areas, including a formal living room and a standout kitchen/dining/family room fitted with modern units, both reception rooms benefiting from gas-fired open fireplaces, and doors opening directly onto the rear garden. There is also a separate utility room, a shower room, and stairs leading down to the basement.

The fully functional cellar provides excellent additional space and is equipped with power, telecoms and heating, making it suitable for a variety of uses.

The first floor comprises three double bedrooms, including the principal bedroom featuring a modern en suite bathroom, walk-in wardrobe, and access to a sun terrace. A spacious family bathroom is also located on this floor. The second floor provides two further double bedrooms and an additional bathroom, developed by the current owners. The loft is boarded and powered, offering useful storage space.
Externally, the property benefits from a gated driveway providing ample off-road parking. The recently landscaped rear garden is level, private, and enjoys a sunny aspect, offering excellent space for outdoor entertaining.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for The Chase. One of many properties for sale in Ascot, Berkshire, SL5 7UJ from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: A beautifully presented detached family home offering a wealth of light and spacious accommodation situated in a gated road in Ascot.
Location: The Chase is a private gated road in Ascot, approximately 1.8 miles away from Ascot High Street and main line station providing services to London (Waterloo).
By road, Ascot is convenient for Heathrow Airport approximately 12.7 miles, M3 (junction 3) approximately 6.4 miles and M25 (junction 13) approximately 12.9 miles.

There are a number of renowned schools in the area including Papplewick, LVS, Hall Grove, Heathfield School, The Marist Schools, Charters, St George's and St Mary's Ascot.. Eton and Wellington colleges area accessible as are the international options of TASIS and ACS Egham.
There are numerous well-known events locally including Royal Ascot, the annual PGA Championship at Wentworth Golf Club and the Cartier International Polo at Smith's lawn.

A wealth of exceptional leisure amenities lie close at hand: Wentworth, Sunningdale, Swinley Forest, Windlesham and The Berkshire golf clubs; polo at Coworth Park, the Royal County of Berkshire Polo Club and Guards Polo Club and horse racing at Ascot and Windsor.
The picturesque Virginia Water Lake and The Savill Garden are both within easy reach, with horse riding at Chobham and Horsell Commons and in Windsor Great Park.
Theatres and cinemas in the area include Cineworld Bracknell, Vue Cinema Camberely, New Victoria Theatre Woking, Yvonne Arnaud Theatre Guildford, Camberley Theatre and Theatre Royal Windsor.

LongDescription.Body:
3 The Chase is a beautifully presented detached family home offering a wealth of light and spacious accommodation situated in a gated road in Ascot.

On entering the property there is a spacious entrance hall which leads to the formal sitting room with a feature fireplace, a guest cloakroom and study which provides a secluded and tranquil work space.

The secondary corridor leads to the superb fully integrated kitchen/dining area which has a central island and works well as a centre to family life with French doors to the rear patio providing a great feeling of space and light. There is access to the utility room and pantry. The family room and hobby room both have doors opening to the rear patio and garden.

An elegant staircase leads up to the galleried first floor landing where there is a principal bedroom suite with dressing room, en suite bathroom and doors to a balcony overlooking the delightful rear garden. Bedroom two also has a dressing area and en suite bathroom and access to the balcony. There are two further bedrooms and a family bathroom.

On the second floor there is a further bedroom with a kitchenette, separate shower room, a gym, an airing cupboard and plant room.

Outside, the property is approached via a block paved in and out driveway with mature hedging and generous parking leading to the double garage with a self contained annexe above.

The self contained annexe comprises a shower room on the ground floor with stairs rising to the first floor where there is a sitting room and stairs to the second floor with a bedroom.

The rear garden has been professionally landscaped and mainly laid to lawn with a variety of well stocked flower borders and a sizeable sun terrace accessed from the kitchen/dining area, hobby room/office and family room making this an ideal space for al fresco dining and entertaining family and friends.

There is a detached studio to the rear of the garden with bi fold doors opening to the delightful garden.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for St. Hughes Close. One of many properties for sale in London, SW17 7UE from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Beautiful two-bedroom end-of-terrace house near Wandsworth Common with private garden and off street parking
Location: St Hughes close is a charming Cul de sac located near the open expanses of Wandsworth Common (0.4 miles), providing ample green spaces ideal for families and pet owners.
Residents enjoy a variety of local shops, with more extensive shopping options available on Bellevue Road (0.4 miles) and Balham High Road (0.8 miles).

Transportation links are excellent, with Wandsworth Common station approximately 0.6 miles away, offering direct services to London Victoria in about 12 minutes. For those preferring underground services, Tooting Bec station on the Northern Line is approximately 0.7 miles away, and Balham Station, offering overground and underground services is approximately 1 mile away facilitating easy access to central London.

LongDescription.Body:
This beautiful two-bedroom end of terrace house is ideally positioned near Wandsworth Common and benefits from a private garden and off street parking for two cars on the driveway, offering a superb blend of comfort, practicality, and modern living. The property benefits from air conditioning on both floors (located in reception and principal bedroom), as well as potential for extending into the loft and side (subject to planning permission).

Upon entry, the modern kitchen is positioned to the left. It offers excellent cupboard storage and is fitted with integrated appliances including an oven, gas cooktop, and refrigerator and washing machine.

To the rear of the property is the bright and spacious reception room, providing generous space for both relaxing and dining. The rear section of the room, currently arranged as the dining area, enjoys an abundance of natural light from overhead skylights. Bi fold doors open directly onto the private south facing garden, creating a seamless flow between indoor and outdoor living. The garden has been thoughtfully designed with shrubbery to the rear and low maintenance turf, making it an ideal space for entertaining and enjoying sunny days.

Upstairs, the accommodation comprises two double bedrooms and a modern family bathroom. The principal bedroom benefits from built in wardrobe space, offering excellent storage.

Additional conveniences include a useful external store beside the front door, a fully boarded loft, and a shed to the left of the house, providing further storage solutions.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Dove Park. One of many properties for sale in Chorleywood, Rickmansworth, Hertfordshire, WD3 5NY from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Attractive family home in a sought-after location
Location: Dove Park is set in an excellent location within this most sought-after residential area of Chorleywood. Chorleywood village centre is approximately one mile away offering good local shopping and Metropolitan Line station providing a frequent rail service to London, Baker Street and The City as well as the main line to Marylebone.

It is one of the Chiltern's most beautiful areas with the picturesque Chorleywood Common with its walks and 9 hole golf course is close by. There is also a good choice of schooling, both state and private including Clement Danes and Chorleywood Primary school
Access to the M25 is at junctions18 and 17.

LongDescription.Body:
This delightful property boasts bright and airy living spaces and offers excellent potential for both modernisation and extension, subject to the usual consents. Offered for sale with no upper chain, it presents a wonderful opportunity to create a bespoke family home in a sought-after location.

The front door opens into a generous entrance hallway, setting the tone for the spacious accommodation that follows. To the front of the property is a charming sitting room featuring a pretty window seat and a central fireplace. To the rear of this room is a cosy nook - ideal for reading or relaxing. The sitting room flows seamlessly into the dual-aspect dining room, which enjoys views over the garden and direct access via double doors - perfect for entertaining during the warmer months.

At the front of the house, a lovely dual-aspect family room provides a welcoming space for informal evenings and benefits from a feature fireplace. Adjacent to the lounge is a practical breakfast room, which leads through an archway to the kitchen. The kitchen is fitted with a range of units and includes an AEG electric hob and oven. A utility area is located at the rear, offering further storage and access to the garden.

First Floor
The principal bedroom benefits from a range of built-in wardrobes and an en suite bathroom. There are three further well-proportioned double bedrooms, all served by a family bathroom. The loft is boarded and accessible via a fitted ladder, offering useful storage space or potential for conversion (subject to planning permission).

Outside
The property is approached via a carriage driveway, providing off-street parking for several vehicles. The front garden features mature hedging and a well-maintained lawn, with gated side access leading to the rear garden.

The beautifully landscaped rear garden includes a spacious terrace with a pergola - perfect for al fresco dining - and is partly enclosed by a handsome brick wall to one side. The remainder of the garden is mainly laid to lawn with mature trees to the rear, as well as a useful summerhouse and greenhouse. The overall plot extends to about 0.3 acres, offering ample space and privacy.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

AdditionalInformation: Directions: From Junction 18 of the M25, exit left onto the A404 towards Chorleywood. Proceed over the first set of traffic lights, at the second turn left into Chorleywood Common Road and continue through the Common and into the village, passing the station on your left and turn left under the railway bridge. Continue straight ahead into Shire Lane and continue left into Heronsgate Road and Neston House is the first house on the corner to the entrance to Dove Park on your left

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Slough Green Lane. One of many properties for sale in Warninglid, Haywards Heath, West Sussex, RH17 5SL from Savills, world leading estate agents.

EpcGallery.Caption: great thorndean farm gables epc (b) (formerly cottage).pdf

Properties.Description: Impressive contemporary country home, beautifully designed with soaring gables and luxurious finishes, in a rural setting.
Location: Great Thorndean Farm Gables is situated on the rural outskirts of Warninglid, a charming West Sussex village with Saxon origins; its centre was the first of six conservation areas in Sussex. The village has a cricket field, a recreation/play area and is home to the Half Moon Pub; a footpath to the south of the property links to a network of footpaths and bridleways giving access to the surrounding countryside.

The larger villages of Cuckfield and Handcross (two miles and three miles respectively) offer a number of local amenities; comprehensive shopping is available at Haywards Heath (five miles), Horsham (eight miles) and Crawley (seven miles).

Sussex offers many leisure activities locally including golf at Mannings Heath, Mid Sussex, Pyecombe and Lindfield clubs, tennis clubs including Wickwoods Country Club, Haywards Heath and Lindfield, sailing at Ardingly Reservoir and Brighton Marina, and a variety of local and county athletics, cricket, rugby and football clubs to suit all ages and abilities. Racing is available at Plumpton, Lingfield and Goodwood, with show jumping at Hickstead. Spa and country house hotels include South Lodge, Ockenden Manor, and Alexander House. Sussex offers a vibrant cultural scene, with world-class opera at Glyndebourne, and the annual Brighton Festival presenting a huge programme of theatre, dance, classical music and literary events.
Haywards Heath offers a fast service to London Bridge and London Victoria, journey time from 41 minutes. The A23/M23 lies to the west, giving access to Gatwick airport, the south coast and the M25.

There are many highly regarded state and private schools in the local area including Cumnor House, Handcross Park, Great Walstead School, Burgess Hill Girls, Lewes Old Grammar and Ardingly, Hurst and Brighton Colleges.
All distances and journey times are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
Built in 2021 on the site of a former farm cottage, and benefitting from the remainder of a 10-year new build warranty, Great Thorndean Farm Gables is a striking contemporary country home, featuring handsome black weatherboarded elevations under a tiled roof. True to its name, the property boasts two soaring gables with full-height glazing into the apex, creating a modern yet harmonious blend with the traditional Sussex vernacular.

Extending to 2,650 square feet, this immaculately presented home is flooded with natural light and offers generous, well-proportioned rooms. The interiors are finished to the highest standards, with neutral décor, tiled flooring through much of the ground floor, and stylish features such as oak internal doors with wrought iron door furniture, plantation blinds, and luxurious Lusso Stone bathroom suites.

A solid wood front door opens into the entrance hall, with the ground floor bedroom accommodation to one side and the open-plan living space to the other. The kitchen and dining room is an expansive and versatile space, with ample space for a large dining table and/or sofas, and features full-height sliding doors leading to the south-facing terrace. The kitchen is superbly appointed, with dark grey shaker-style cabinetry, Silestone worktops, a wood-block-topped central island with breakfast bar, and appliances including a Mercury range oven with five-ring induction hob, a Bosch dishwasher, undercounter fridges, freezer, wine fridge, and a copper Capel sink.

A step descends to the elegant triple-aspect sitting room, which also benefits from full-height sliding doors, exposed timbers, and a modern Morso woodburner, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere.

The ground floor also offers two well-appointed bedrooms, both with French doors opening to the terrace on the west side of the house, and a stylish shower room with a large wet-room style shower. Additionally, there is a cloakroom and a utility room equipped with space for laundry appliances.

A staircase from the dining area rises to a spacious first-floor landing with a skylight, ideal for use as a study. The two bedroom suites on this floor are particularly impressive, featuring stunning vaulted ceilings with exposed timbers and full-height glazing that extends into the gable ends. The principal suite includes a large walk-in dressing room with custom shelving and hanging rails, an en suite bathroom with a freestanding bath and separate shower, and French doors opening to a Juliet balcony. The second bedroom suite has an en suite shower room and French doors leading to a balcony with a glazed balustrade.

Outside
The property is approached via a double five-bar gate that opens onto a large bonded gravel driveway and parking area. Elevated from the lane, the house makes a striking first impression.

The garden lies primarily to the west and south of the house, laid mainly to lawn with a large stone-paved terrace extending around two sides. A wooden post and rail fence partially divides the garden from the paddock, which stretches away from the house to the south west, bordered by mature trees and hedgerows, as indicated on the site plan.

In all, about 7.7 acres.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

The gabled design, vaulted ceilings, and full-height glazing create a home that feels both dramatic and welcoming, and the 7.7 acres of grounds make it perfect for anyone seeking a true country lifestyle

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Knowle Lane. One of many properties for sale in Cranleigh, Surrey, GU6 8JW from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Charming character home with mature gardens in an idyllic location.
Location: Old Barn Cottage is conveniently situated in Cranleigh, known as England's largest village and offers extensive facilities often found in a small market town. Within Cranleigh itself are many local independent shops and supermarkets, including an M&S food hall. Cranleigh Arts Centre hosts regular films, theatre, bands, comedy and children's shows.

A complete range of shopping, leisure and cultural amenities is to be found at Guildford approximately 10 miles away, where there is also a mainline station offering a fast and frequent service to London Waterloo with journey times from 32 minutes.
The nearby Surrey Hills is a designated Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty and includes some of the most beautiful countryside in the southeast.
Cranleigh has a very good selection of schools within easy reach including Cranleigh School, Royal Grammar, Guildford High School, St Catherine's, Longacre and Charterhouse.

Recreational opportunities include golf at several local clubs; Cranleigh's own Sport and Leisure centre, polo at Ewhurst, racing at Epsom and Sandown Park, and country sports at a number of local venues. In addition, the surrounding countryside offers excellent walking, cycling and riding directly from the property. Of note, the Downs Link which follows the old railway line can be readily accessed by the public footpath from the drive.

LongDescription.Body:
Old Barn Cottage represents a characterful village house originally dating from 1650 and was sympathetically converted in the 1950's. The current owners have been in residence for over forty years and the property offers excellent lateral living space with a flexible arrangement of accommodation that extends to approximately 2,500 sq ft.

There is a superb open plan kitchen with doors opening on to the patio and gardens beyond. The bespoke kitchen features a central island, contemporary fitted cabinetry and integrated appliances. The sitting room boasts superb proportions, an attractive brick surround fireplace and a gas fireplace. For more formal entertaining is the dining room with beautiful parquet flooring.

Also on the ground floor are two/three bedrooms with convenient access to both the family bathroom and the shower room. This configuration lends itself to multigenerational living or visiting guests. Additionally there is a music room which is versatile in its use, a boot room and a utility room.

Further bedroom accommodation can be found on the first floor. There is a significant, dual aspect principal bedroom with built-in wardrobes and a large en suite shower room. There are two further bedrooms on this floor serviced by another family bathroom.

Outside, the gardens are beautifully presented with two charming seating areas, a pergola, a well maintained lawn and well stocked flowering beds providing an array of stunning colours throughout the seasons. At the end of the garden is a delightful ornamental pond. There is a former stable block with four boxes and an adjoining workshop.

The Old Granary is a delightful part refurbished outbuilding retaining many of its original features which would make an ideal home office, games room or further workshop area.

The property is accessed via electric gates and a long, sweeping private driveway. Old Barn Cottage benefits from an integral double garage, separate single garage and courtyard parking with space for multiple vehicles.

View payable Stamp Duty for this property

Properties.QuoteText: I love the character features at Old Barn Cottage, the flexibility of the accommodation as well as the wonderful kitchen with its open garden access.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Manor Lane. One of many properties for sale in Abbots Leigh, North Somerset, BS8 3RU from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: A striking split-level detached home with panoramic views across the North Somerset countryside.
Location: Perfectly placed for both peace and convenience, the property offers excellent access to fashionable Clifton and the commercial city centre while embracing the calm of the countryside.
There are good schools , preparatory and secondary available from Wraxall and Clifton. For sporting endeavours, there are golf courses, health clubs and a myriad of rambling trails to keep you fit.
Access to the arterial roads connecting to the M5 are in proximity and there are  train stations serving the wider country in Nailsea and Backwell (16.9 miles) and Temple Meads (5.2 miles).

LongDescription.Body:
Monks Bench is an exceptional 1960s detached family home, tucked away on one of the area's most sought-after roads. This elegant split-level residence sits proudly among beautifully curated gardens and enjoys some of the most commanding views, stretching from the surrounding countryside to the Bristol Channel and even the distant Welsh hills.

Lovingly maintained and updated over the last 15 years, the current owners have enhanced the home while preserving its timeless charm. There is still ample scope for the next custodian to reimagine the space and make it truly their own.

Constructed with rendered elevations and double glazing throughout, the home features expansive open-plan living spaces designed to bring in natural light and frame the views from every angle.

Step inside via a solid wood front door into a welcoming reception hall. From here, the wide staircase leads up to the heart of the home, a generous landing with free-flowing living areas that lead directly off and combine relaxation, dining, and entertaining. The drawing room, L-shaped in design, offers various lounging zones and opens directly into a sun-drenched conservatory and outdoor sun deck, perfect for soaking in those majestic southerly vistas and enjoying the sunsets.

Adjacent, the large dining room connects to a contemporary, fully fitted kitchen, which in turn flows into a second conservatory, an ideal spot for morning coffee while overlooking the landscaped front garden.

The principal bedroom is a luxurious retreat, complete with a stylish ensuite bathroom and fitted wardrobes. A guest bedroom enjoys access to a Jack & Jill shower room, while a third bedroom, also ensuite, sits on the lower level near the utility and garage, making it perfect as a private guest suite.
Outside, the home is approached via secure electric gates leading to generous off-street parking and a sizable under-house garage with electric doors. Adjoining this are a large workshop, boiler room, and cloakroom.

The gardens wrap around the property on three sides. Properties.Description: Fabulous family home with landscaped gardens within an gated development
Location: King Edward Mews is a gated development situated in the village of Newport, which is home to a train station on the London Liverpool Street link (journeys taking from an hour),  a leisure centre with a gym, tennis court, sports hall and astro turf, a doctor's surgery and a village hall.

The medieval market town of Saffron Walden is 3.5 miles away and offers a wide array of amenities, including Saffron Hall and Cinema and the popular market which takes place every Tuesday and Saturday, with access to Audley End House and Gardens.

Newport has Newport Primary School and Joyce Frankland Academy for secondary school students. There are also further independent schools available for all ages in Cambridge, 18.1 miles away, including The Perse, The Leys, St Faiths to name a few.
All distances and times are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
Set within an exclusive gated development in the heart of Newport, this elegant family home combines generous proportions with modern design and landscaped gardens, offering over 3,000 sq ft of versatile living space.

The house opens into a bright hallway that leads you towards the principal living areas. To one side, a formal sitting room with a feature fireplace and large bifold doors with direct access to the rear terrace, offering great views of the garden. The dining room enjoys a bay window to the front, creating a welcoming spot for family gatherings. The rear of the property is designed with modern family life in mind  a large open-plan kitchen and living area finished with sleek cabinetry, a central island and a full suite of integrated appliances and has a set of bi-fold doors and French doors opening directly to the terrace and garden. From the kitchen there is also access to a useful utility room and into the integral double garage. A cloak room completes the downstairs accommodation.

Upstairs, the first floor offers five generous bedrooms. The principal suite is especially impressive, with a large bedroom, a private dressing room and a stylish en suite bathroom with bath and separate shower cubicle. The second and third bedrooms also benefit from their own en suite, while the remaining bedrooms are served by a modern family bathroom. Each room is well-proportioned and filled with light.

The landscaped garden has been designed for year-round enjoyment, with a stone terrace, lawn and a detached garden room that could serve as a home office or studio. To the front, the property has ample parking and a double garage, with secure gated access to the development.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Heath Green, Forest Road. One of many properties for sale in Tarporley, Cheshire, CW6 0JD from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Located within a 10-minute stroll of the highly desirable Tarporley village, properties with this location, specification and lifestyle dynamic seldom come on to the market.
Location: Nearby Tarporley is a highly sought-after village with an attractive High Street offering a diverse selection of amenities including cafes, restaurants, public houses, independent shops, fashion boutiques and three churches, as well as primary and secondary schooling and a range of community services.

The City of Chester (12 miles) is one of the north west's leading retail and commercial centres serving a catchment area extending from Manchester to Shrewsbury and covering the whole of North Wales. It is home to Chester Racecourse, the oldest site of continuous horse racing in the UK.

The nearby leisure opportunities are excellent. Portal Golf Club and Carden Park Hotel and Spa both have championship golf courses and a range of other facilities. There are also numerous walks and cycling routes in the area including Delamere Forest and the Sandstone Trail, as well as polo at the Cheshire Polo Ground.

Chester also gives access to the motorway network providing a link to other northwest and midlands conurbations and to Liverpool (25 miles) and Manchester (27 miles) airports. Crewe Railway Station (17 miles) offers regular rail service to London Euston (from about 1 hour 30 minutes).

There are a number of highly regarded schools in the locality including St Peter's Primary School in Duddon, The Grange in Hartford, Kings, Queens and Abbeygate in Chester along with High Schools in both Tarporley and Christleton.
The Cheshire landscape, Welsh mountains, nearby country pubs and restaurants and varied local architecture all give the area a truly unique feel.
Distances

Tarporley 1 mile | Nantwich 12 miles | Chester 12 miles | Kings School Chester 15 miles | Crewe Railway Station 17 miles | M6 (Junction 20) 17 miles | Liverpool Airport 25 miles | Manchester Airport 27 miles

LongDescription.Body:
Hill Farm House sits proudly in exclusive private grounds spanning approximately 1.5 acres yet less than a mile from Tarporley village. With breath-taking elevated views of five counties and spectacular sunsets from the many Indian paved terraces, this is the type of lifestyle' property that seldom comes to the market.

Sitting behind electric gates at the end of a no through road, the impressive Mediterranean style courtyard entrance offers triple garaging and off-road parking for several vehicles and frames the entrance to this secluded executive family home. Extending to approximately 5,700 sq. ft Hill Farm House has been completely remodelled and extended over the last few years to offer a well thought through yet practical and stylish home.

To the ground floor there are four reception rooms, particular note must be made of the garden room with its' floor to ceiling windows which flood the room with natural light and offers spectacular views of the landscaped grounds, Beeston & Peckforton castles and the Welsh mountains beyond. The principal living spaces all with scenic views are centred around a large welcoming entrance hall. The formal drawing room extends to 26'10ft with an open fireplace and alcove seating. Opposite is the dual aspect panelled dining room, next to which is the bright and spacious recently remodelled kitchen with its far-reaching views across the terraces and gardens beyond. There is a large central island, a host of built in appliances, a separate breakfast station, larder, storage and a separate utility room. To the rear of the property there is a further living room/snug, a small home office and access to the integral triple garaging with its W.C. and large storage workshop. The loft space above the triple garage has been converted into a versatile entertainment room that could fulfil a variety of uses.

The first floor has five extremely well-proportioned double bedrooms, three of which are en-suite, plus a family bathroom and the option of a sixth bedroom or nursery, dependent upon the purchaser's requirements. Undeniably, the principal bedroom suite makes a wonderful statement. A beautiful bay window with extraordinary, framed views sets the tone for a relaxing morning coffee to start the day. Combined with a walk-in dressing room and stunning remodelled contemporary en-suite bathroom, this suite will no doubt leave a lasting impression.

With a south-westerly perspective, the sun shines on the abundance of gardens and terraces from sunrise to sunset. This enviable aspect creates an entertaining dynamic and lifestyle opportunity that very few properties could ever match. One of the properties outstanding features is unquestionably the quality of landscaping, a fusion of eye-catching mature palm trees, framed by traditional box hedging throughout the gardens which results in a fine country house feel with a unique Mediterranean twist.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Westminster Road. One of many properties for sale in York, North Yorkshire, YO30 6LY from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: A refurbished and extended three-bedroom semi-detached house with open-plan living and a south-facing garden, set in the Clifton Conservation Area close to the city centre and station.
Location: Clifton is a distinguished, tree-lined residential district located just beyond York's historic city walls and is regarded as one of the city's most prestigious addresses. Characterised by elegant Georgian and Victorian townhouses, the area forms part of the wider Clifton Conservation Area.

Westminster Road is quietly positioned away from the main thoroughfare and is within comfortable walking distance of Bootham School, The Minster School and St Olave's School, while being immediately adjacent to St Peter's School.

The property enjoys a particularly attractive pedestrian route into the city centre, either through the historic Bootham Bar or via a scenic riverside walk, providing easy access to York Minster and the city's vibrant array of shops, restaurants and cultural attractions.
There are a number of excellent green spaces nearby, including Homestead Park, Clifton and Rawcliffe Ings, and the renowned Museum Gardens, all offering delightful walking opportunities.

Clifton Green serves as a well-regarded local hub, offering a café, gift shop, sandwich shop, butcher, delicatessen, convenience store, as well as dental and chiropractic practices. The recently refurbished Old Grey Mare public house is now home to Brew York Craft Beer and YUZU Street Food, while the nearby YHA York offers a café and garden. The area benefits from a strong sense of community and an active neighbourhood atmosphere.

For commuters, York railway station is easily accessible via a short and pleasant walk over Scarborough Bridge, providing regular mainline services to London King's Cross in under two hours, as well as to Leeds, Manchester and Edinburgh.
The local road network lies just to the north, with the A1237 providing convenient access to the A64 and wider regional routes.
Please note that all distances and journey times are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
Situated in one of York's most sought-after residential areas, this beautifully refurbished and extended semi-detached house offers well-balanced, contemporary accommodation arranged over two floors.

The property is entered via a welcoming hallway which leads to an elegant sitting room featuring a log-burning stove set within a tiled hearth. To the rear, the impressive open-plan kitchen and dining room forms the heart of the home, fitted with a range of modern units and incorporating an integrated dishwasher and a freestanding Smeg range cooker. Sliding glazed doors flood the space with natural light and provide a seamless connection to the south-facing garden. A separate utility room offers valuable ancillary accommodation, complemented by a ground floor WC.

The first floor comprises three well-proportioned bedrooms, served by a stylish house bathroom featuring a separate walk-in shower.

Externally, the property benefits from off-street parking on a recently laid resin driveway, with additional bike and log storage to the side. The rear garden is designed for ease of maintenance, laid predominantly to lawn and bordered by an established evergreen hedge. A newly constructed timber pergola with patio provides an ideal setting for outdoor entertaining and al fresco dining.

We wish to inform prospective purchasers of this property that the seller is a Savills employee.

Photos taken in 2023.

Properties.Description: An outstanding period barn conversion occupying an idyllic setting, with fabulous views across the Washburn Valley
Location: Set in an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, approximately eight miles to the west of Harrogate. The area combines the advantage of a particularly peaceful setting with easy access to the surrounding towns of Harrogate, Otley and Ilkley, all offering a superb range of facilities and excellent schooling. The West Yorkshire business centres of Leeds and Bradford are both within approximately fifteen miles. Leeds/Bradford International Airport and railway stations at Weeton and Menston are all within about a twenty minute drive.
Please note all distances are approximate.
Services: Mains electricity and water, bio mass boiler for heating and hot water, Drainage via a compliant treatment plant.

LongDescription.Body:
Norwood Barn is a traditional stone barn typical of the Washburn Valley, which the present owners acquired some 10 years ago and began an ambitious project to covert the buildings into a modern family home designed blend into the landscape and exploit the magnificent scenery.

With living accommodation extending to just over 3,368 square feet, arranged over two floors with the potential to create a one bedroom annexe, the property is both versatile and practical, well suited to families expecting regular guests.

Entering the property the quality of finish is immediately apparent, a spacious open plan breakfast kitchen is fitted with bespoke wooden cabinetry and quality integrated appliances. The central island unit has a breakfast bar with seating for four people and there is space for a seating area and kitchen table. A vaulted ceiling with exposed wooden beams creates a real atmosphere in this light and airy space, perfect for day to day living. A useful separate utility sits just off from the kitchen.

Continuing through the ground floor there are two sizeable reception rooms, both of which benefit from multi-fuel stoves and French doors giving superb vistas across the gardens and valley beyond. The ground floor also benefits from a bedroom suite, ideal for guests or those requiring accessible accommodation.

Bedroom four sits on a mezzanine level above the family room and would therefore lend itself to conversion into a one bedroom annexe for multi-generational living or guests.

The first floor houses an impressive principle bedroom suite with a dressing room leading to a well-appointed en suite.
Bedrooms two and three are serviced by the house bathroom.

Externally the property benefits from a number of stone-built outbuildings, one housing the bio-mass boiler and the rest providing ideal storage space. A private tarmac driveway provides parking for several cars.

The garden has been beautifully designed with a large stone paved seating terrace flanking the entire ground floor, ideal for enjoying the south and west facing gardens. The garden is primarily set to lawn, flanked with mature trees and shrubbery, making for a sizeable yet low maintenance garden perfect for entertaining during the summer months.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Oxford Road. One of many properties for sale in Frilford, Abingdon, Oxfordshire, OX13 5NR from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Imposing and spacious new build home.
Location: Rosewood House is located in the hamlet of Frilford, less than 3 miles from the thriving market town of Abingdon and 9 miles south west of Oxford. Frilford has excellent transport links including public buses to Oxford and Didcot Railway Station. The neighbouring village of Frilford Heath has a popular pub and a Championship Golf Course. The area also offers convenient access to some superb schools including the sought after Abingdon Prep which is a short walk from the house.

LongDescription.Body:
Rosewood House is an imposing home providing just under 5000 sq. ft of living accommodation, featuring a comfortable kitchen/dining/family room, utility room, sitting room, snug and study. To the first floor, there is a superb principal bedroom with dressing room and en suite, four further bedrooms - two with en suite - and a family bathroom. Rosewood House is set in a mature plot of approximately 0.4 acres that backs onto woodland and an open brook. There is ample driveway parking that leads to a detached double garage with electric roller doors.

The property features a fantastic double height entrance hall, with a fabulous oak bifurcated staircase and central roof lantern that ensures the space is flooded with natural light.

Double doors from the entrance hall open into a full-length kitchen/living/dining area with bay window to the front and full width bi-fold doors to the rear of the house. An individually designed, German made kitchen is paired with quartz work surfaces and oak accents, a fully integrated suite of Siemens appliances and a Quooker hot tap.

A comfortable sitting room has bi-fold doors that open onto the patio and gardens, whilst the separate snug has a fireplace with contemporary multi-fuel burning stove. Completing the accommodation on the ground floor is a quiet study, convenient utility room, plant room and WC.

To the first floor, the principal bedroom features a walk-in dressing area and en suite shower room with double basin washstand. The guest bedroom also has a dressing area and a stunning ensuite shower room, whilst of the three remaining bedrooms, one has an ensuite, the others are served by a family bathroom.

Rosewood House has a detached double garage with driveway parking for several vehicles. The generous gardens offer a range of established trees which ensure a good degree of privacy.

Each property is equipped with state-of-the-art technology. The central heating is provided by an air source heat pump, supplemented by a whole-house heat recovery system and ventilation. A large unvented hot water cylinder ensures plenty of hot water, while a thermostatically controlled wet underfloor heating system offers comfort on both ground and first floors, with individual programmable zone control. The properties are equipped with low-energy LED downlighting in warm white, pendant lighting, and CAT6 data wiring throughout. Phone and TV points are available in all reception rooms and bedrooms. Safety features include mains-linked heat and smoke detection, as well as the provision of an audio-visual entry control system with two internal interfaces. There is also exteriors lighting to the patios, porches, garaging, and driveways.

Properties.Description: Silver Birches is a brand new, immaculately finished, turnkey, four-bedroom tile hung detached gated house with beautiful rear garden, centred in the Surrey Hills in the heart of Peaslake.
Location: Peaslake is located in the heart of the Surrey Hills, a designated Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, an Area of Great Landscape Value and within the Peaslake Conservation Area.

The village enjoys a vibrant social life with many clubs and societies; there is the parish church, inn, village hall and a general stores and Post Office. Further convenience shopping and farm shops are available at Abinger Hammer (2.2 miles), Shere (1.5 miles) and Forest Green (4.8 miles).

A more extensive range of amenities is available at Cranleigh (4.7 miles), whilst the full range of shopping, leisure and cultural amenities may be found at Guildford (9.8 miles), Dorking (7.7 miles) and Horsham (14.9 miles).

There is an excellent choice of schools in the area, including Peaslake School, Shere Infants, Duke of Kent, Hurtwood House, Cranleigh, Charterhouse and St Teresa's. Recreational opportunities include walking, cycling and riding in the surrounding countryside.
Please note, all distances and times are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
Silver Birches is a brand new, beautifully crafted four bedroom detached home set behind secure electric gates in the heart of Peaslake, within the picturesque Surrey Hills. Designed and finished to an exceptional standard, this turnkey property combines timeless village architecture with modern luxury, offering an ideal retreat for those seeking both style and tranquillity.

On the ground floor, an elegant entrance hall leads to a generous sitting room at the front of the home, complemented by a separate studyperfect for home working.

The rear of the house is dedicated to an impressive open plan kitchen, dining, and reception room, enhanced by full width sliding glass doors that flood the room with natural light and open directly onto the beautifully landscaped rear garden. The Italian designed kitchen features high end appliances and luxurious finishes, while Italian porcelain floor tiles and underfloor heating provide comfort and sophistication throughout the ground level. A practical utility room off the kitchen and a guest cloakroom complete the layout.

The first floor offers three well-proportioned bedrooms, including a principal bedroom with its own en suite bathroom and built in wardrobes. Two further bedrooms share a beautifully well-appointed family bathroom fitted with high quality Italian sanitary ware, ensuring a consistent standard of luxury across every room.

The top floor is dedicated to a bedroom suite, featuring a spacious double bedroom, a dressing room, and an en suite bathroom. With views over the surrounding greenery, it offers an inviting and peaceful retreat for family or visitors.

The rear garden has been thoughtfully landscaped to create an inviting and versatile outdoor space that makes the most of its idyllic surroundings. Flowing directly from the impressive kitchen and reception area, a wide porcelain tiled terrace provides an elegant setting for outdoor dining and entertaining. From here, a central staircase leads up to the expansive lawn, framed by newly planted borders and enclosed with attractive timber fencing.

The elevated aspect of the garden offers open views across the adjoining fields, enhancing the sense of space and privacy while giving the garden a wonderfully peaceful, rural backdrop. Whether for family activities, quiet relaxation, or hosting guests, this beautifully finished garden delivers both practicality and tranquillity in equal measure.

Built to the latest Building Regulations, Silver Birches delivers excellent energy efficiency with high insulation standards, underfloor heating throughout, and an air source heat pump for environmentally conscious living. Additional benefits include a 10 year structural warranty, a full security alarm system, and the privacy and reassurance of electric entry gates.

With its blend of craftsmanship, contemporary comforts, and an idyllic village setting, Silver Birches represents an exceptional opportunity to acquire a truly turnkey home in one of Surrey's most desirable locations.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for East Road. One of many properties for sale in St George's Hill, Weybridge, Surrey, KT13 0LD from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: An exceptional new residence of timeless elegance in the heart of St Georges Hill.
Location: St George's Hill is internationally renowned as one of the most sought-after private estates in England.  Access on to the private, gated estate is controlled 24 hours a day by the estate's security personnel and is restricted at all times. St George's Hill is set in some 900 acres and features a private championship standard golf course and a separate private tennis and squash club.  The tennis club is one of the foremost racquet sports clubs in England, with 30 grass and all-weather tennis courts, two indoor courts and four squash courts.  The tennis club also has a 20 metre indoor swimming pool, a well-equipped gym and a restaurant and bar.

In addition to the on-site sporting facilities, there are many other challenging golf courses locally including Burhill, Wisley, Woking, Foxhills, Queenwood, Worplesdon and West Hill.  Racing is available at Sandown Park, Ascot, Epsom and Windsor.  Polo can be enjoyed at Guards Polo Club and The Royal Berkshire Polo Club.

St George's Hill offers an ideal location for families looking for security and privacy and there are several English and International schools in the area including Reeds School, St George's College and The ACS International School at Cobham. Weybridge and the surrounding area has a variety of Ofsted rated good and outstanding primary and senior schools in both the private and public sectors, providing education from nursery through to 6th form and also a very popular 6th form college.

Located on the South bank of the rivers Thames and Wey where the two rivers meet, Weybridge is an historic town that dates back to the Iron Age. Originally named after the wooden bridge over the river Wey used by pilgrims to the Chertsey Abbey, in the last 100 years Weybridge has become known for its aspirational, innovative and creative residents. Each generation leaving their legacy to the town through its own style of properties and industry.

There was once a royal palace in Weybridge that was used by Henry VIII and his wives, as well as other Kings and Queens. Today, Oatlands Park Hotel is all that remains of the royal estate. You can still find reminders of the original palace in and around Weybridge.

In more recent history, Weybridge was a magnet for motor enthusiasts, as it was home to Brooklands Race Track, some of the track still remains. The river Wey cuts through the Brooklands site. If you wanted to catch some racing without paying, you would take a punt up the river. This was apparently the origin of the phrase punters. Today you can visit Brooklands Museum and Mercedes Benz World at the site.
Privacy, space and accessibility, an abundance of sports and activities and excellent schools makes Weybridge the perfect location for those who enjoy a variety of interests and passions.

There are many water sports on the Thames: paddle boarding; kayaking; rowing; sailing and wild water swimming are among the most popular. Weybridge also has many clubs for children and adults: tennis; rugby; football; creative arts; horse riding; dance and more.

With several restaurants, pubs and local markets as well as independent and chain cafés the town is well served for a variety of excellent food and drink, just right for your formal and informal occasions. With cinemas, theatres, local art galleries, museums and historic houses and gardens nearby, there is never a shortage of things to do.
The town is near junction 11 of the M25 and is on a fast train line into London Waterloo. Journey time from 31 minutes.
Please note all distances are approximate.

LongDescription.Body:
Set within the prestigious private St George's Hill Estate in Weybridge, this exceptional newly built residence has been crafted by Park Lane Developments, trusted creators of several beautifully crafted homes across the estate. Combining classic architectural grandeur with sophisticated contemporary interiors, the property is approached through imposing gates and a sweeping driveway, making an immediate impression with its striking red brick facade and perfect symmetry.

The moment you step inside you are welcomed by a magnificent double height reception hall centred beneath an elegant mezzanine style landing that allows natural light to pour into the space from above. This breath taking area is beautifully finished with detailed panelling, wide plank flooring and sightlines that draw the eye through to the landscaped gardens beyond. A sweeping staircase rises gracefully to the galleried first floor landing.

Designed for both luxurious everyday living and large scale entertaining, the ground floor offers a superb flow of generously proportioned rooms.
The formal drawing room and elegant dining room enjoy front to back natural light while a beautifully appointed study provides a calm, private workspace with views of the grounds.

At the heart of the home lies an outstanding kitchen, breakfast and family room: a true showpiece. Featuring bespoke cabinetry, a vast central island, premium appliances and stunning coffered ceilings; this space opens seamlessly to the gardens through full height glazing. An adjoining pantry provides excellent additional storage and preparation space ideal for entertaining.
A separate family room, bar, cloakroom and well planned utility areas further enhance the ground floor layout.

Upstairs the galleried landing leads to five beautifully presented bedroom suites each offering exceptional privacy.
The principal suite is truly impressive, an expansive bedroom with multiple French doors to a private terrace, a sumptuous bathroom with a central freestanding bath framed by full height windows, complemented by two luxurious marble effect showers and extensive dressing areas. The further bedroom suites each feature bespoke wardrobes and elegant ensuite bathrooms finished to the highest standards.

The rear elevation of the house opens onto a large terrace overlooking the pristine lawn and striking outdoor swimming pool. A covered seating area with classical columns provides the perfect setting for alfresco dining and relaxation. The gardens offer both privacy and tranquillity framed by mature trees.

The property benefits from two separate double garages.
The first is a striking glazed showcase style garage ideal for displaying a car collection with large windows that frame the vehicles inside.
The second double garage forms part of a larger building which incorporates superb self contained staff accommodation above. This private apartment includes a well appointed kitchenette, a spacious bedroom and a modern ensuite bathroom offering excellent flexibility for live in staff, guests or extended family while remaining independent from the main house.

Other nearby schools include St Joseph's Catholic Primary, Oakley C of E Combined School, John Watson School, and Lord William's Secondary School. For private education, Ashford Prep School is in nearby Dorton, while Oxford offers top independent schools such as Magdalen College, Oxford High, Radley College, Headington, and Dragon School, along with the renowned University of Oxford.

Rectory Woods offers excellent connectivity. Haddenham & Thame Parkway station, just outside Thame, provides direct trains to London Marylebone and Oxford, with Birmingham less than 90 minutes away. The M40 is just 3.1 miles away at junction 8A. Local buses, with a stop only 200 yards from Rectory Woods, offer regular routes to Thame, Aylesbury, and Oxford.

Properties.Description: The Alder offers an expansive kitchen family rooms that spans the entire width of the home with bi fold doors opening out to the rear garden., Call now for more details.
Location: Located between the market towns of Great Dunmow and Braintree, Felsted is a picturesque village surrounded by several hamlets within the North Essex countryside.   At its heart is one of the UK's leading public schools, which dates back to 1564 and has inspired the village since its inception with its impressive architecture and perfectly manicured cricket pitches.

Felsted boasts a variety of amenities, which includes a village store, a coffee shop and a tearoom (for books and music), a ladies boutique clothes shop and two village pubs, The Swan Inn and The Chequers.  There are a wealth of clubs and societies and a vibrant community spirit within the village, many taking place within the Village Hall.

The area provides access to miles of footpaths: the Flitch Way, a former railway line that passes through 15 miles of rural Essex countryside provides an ideal route to jog, dog-walk or horse-ride, while nearby Great Notley Country Park and Hatfield Forest offer a wealth of leisure and green spaces.   There are further opportunities within the area for horse-riding lessons, a choice of golf courses, and superb sports and fitness facilities at Felsted School.

Felsted Gate's location offers excellent connectivity, with easy access to the A120 and proximity to the M11, making London and Cambridge accessible for commuters. Stansted Airport is also nearby, providing international travel options. The village of Felsted itself boasts a strong community spirit, renowned schools such as Felsted Independent School, and a range of local amenities, including shops, pubs, and eateries.

LongDescription.Body:
The Alder features a magnificent open plan kitchen/dining and family room with large bi-fold doors opening straight onto the rear garden.

Offering a wealth of living space, this home also benefits from a separate living room and dining room.

To the first floor, there are four large double bedrooms including a stunning principal bedroom, which benefits from a stylish en suite with bath tub and separate shower. The second bedroom also enjoys its own en suite shower room whilst the family bathroom serves the remaining two bedrooms.

Externally there is a double garage with driveway parking.

Properties.Description: A meticulously refurbished detached family house in very good location just 0.16km from station

LongDescription.Body:
Situated in the sought-after Bower Hill area of Epping, this detached home has been thoughtfully refurbished throughout and offers an impressive 3,721.8 sqft of well-designed accommodation.

Blending modern comfort with a sense of character, the property provides four generous double bedrooms and six beautifully appointed bath and shower rooms, making it an excellent option for families or buyers seeking a long term home.

The house is presented to an exceptional standard, featuring high quality floor tiling, a stylish kitchen fitted with Siemens appliances, gas fires and elegant marble worktops with a matching curved breakfast bar.

Each of the six bathroom suites is finished in classic white with attractive tiling, while the spacious open plan kitchen and family room includes bi-folding doors and a discreet popup TVideal for both entertaining and everyday living.

The kitchen itself offers a comprehensive range of integrated appliances, including multiple ovens, a wide hob, a designer extractor fan, fridge freezer and wine cooler.

A striking hexagonal roof window illuminates the galleried landing and reception hall, creating a bright and welcoming atmosphere.

The property benefits from numerous upgrades, including an air source heat pump for hot water and underfloor heating, a water softener, triple glazing and air conditioning in the principal bedroom.

A built in Sonos sound system with concealed speakers serves the ground floor and patio areas, enhancing the home's contemporary feel.

Externally, the home enjoys a triple length garage and ample block paved parking behind secure electric gates. The gardens are beautifully maintained, with external lighting creating an inviting ambience and an irrigation system ensuring effortless upkeep.

Additional security features include a 24hour CCTV monitoring system and lockable side gates, offering peace of mind.

MetaInformation.Description: Property details for Ochre Meadows. One of many new homes for sale in The Green, Theale, Berkshire, RG7 5DR from Savills, world leading estate agents.

Properties.Description: Ochre Meadows in Theale presents a range of 3, 4 and 5 bedroom houses in a variety of complementary architectural styles and superbly located to allow for ease of access by road and rail
Location: Conveniently located for Theale's amenities, with easy access to the surrounding countryside and the Chiltern Hills Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty, Ochre Meadows offers a wonderful opportunity for quintessential village living combined with excellent connections. Reading is just six miles from the development while Theale station is less than a mile away and the M4 only a three minute drive, so you can enjoy fast, direct travel links to London and beyond

Theale is a vibrant Berkshire village, situated next to the Kennet and Avon Canal. Its historic high street is lined with shops, services, cafés and restaurants, while its amenities include sports clubs, a library, village hall, schools and recreation grounds, all contributing to a friendly and active community for all ages.

There are four pubs in the village, giving you plenty of choice for a relaxed meal or evening drinks with friends. Theale Golf Club is also an attractive spot for coffee or lunch, with views over its immaculate 18-hole course. Just down the road, you'll discover the delightful Cobbs Farm Shop and Café, where you can shop for locally sourced products and enjoy a visit to the café. When it is time to get active, there is Woodfield Way Recreation Ground with it wide open spaces, Theale Recreation Ground with a skate park, playground, football and cricket pitches, and scenic walks or bike rides along the Kennet and Avon canal.

A settlement since the Bronze Age, Theale today is a thriving village, combining its own distinct character and community with the convenience of proximity to Reading and the M4 tech-corridor'. A prime location on the main road to Bath and Bristol, Theale became a major stopping point for coach travel; by 1840 there were 11 inns on the high street and a plethora of local businesses serving the needs of travellers.

The nearby Englefield Estate dates back to 871AD. The stunning gardens around the Elizabethan manor house are available to visit, while the surrounding parkland  features miles of footpaths and bridleways. With woodland, lakes and ponds, it is a beautiful place to explore.

When it is time for a change of pace, head for Reading. With shopping, sports venues, bars, restaurants and entertainment, as well as museums, monuments and the River Thames, it offers plenty of ways to spend your free time. Career opportunities are equally impressive, with a diverse range of prestigious employers locally.
For regal splendour and heritage, Windsor is just over 30 minutes away by car, while Newbury, a market town with a burgeoning arts scene, theatre and famous racecourse, is also
within easy reach.

Theale is perfectly placed to explore Berkshire's countryside, including the North Wessex Downs. The Moor Copse Nature Reserve is two miles away, or head to The Living Rainforest and Bucklebury Farm & Deer Safari Park for a fantastic family outing. There are several golf clubs, gyms and leisure centres, all within a 10-minute drive.

Living at Ochre Meadows you will be fortunate enough to have the Theale C of E Primary School & Nursery and Tigers at Theale Day Nursery within walking distance. For older children, Theale Green Secondary School is less than half a mile away. Several of Reading and the surrounding area's schools and colleges are also a short drive, bus or bicycle ride away, giving you even more choice.

Despite its village setting, Ochre Meadows is incredibly well connected, with fast direct trains to London Paddington from Theale station, which is just a 15-minute walk or a 2-minute drive away. The M4 is also easily accessible, only a 3-minute drive (1.3 miles) from the development. Additionally, The Green bus stop in Theale is conveniently situated parallel to the development, providing even more transport options. It is ideally situated for relaxed country living, with the buzz of town and city life available whenever you want it.
*All distances and times according to Google Maps

**Architectural details and elevational treatments, including window or porch styles and positions, may vary from those shown; bay windows are plot specific. All dimensions are approximate. All imagery, including trees and landscaping, are illustrative. Please check with one of our Sales Consultants for detailed information regarding your chosen home style and/or plot. We continually review the specification of our product and reserve the right to amend it. Although it is our intention that information given regarding garages, car ports and parking is truthful, these aspects are subject to change as Ochre Meadows progresses.

LongDescription.Body:
OCHRE MEADOWS

Situated in the thriving Berkshire village of Theale, Ochre Meadows is a collection of 3, 4 and 5 bedroom homes thoughtfully designed to reflect the local architecture, with a central green, play area and orchard creating space for all the community to use. With Theale High Street just a short walk away, residents will feel connected to this established village, and enjoy the tranquil leafy surroundings of this sought-after location.

Ochre Meadows enjoys a verdant setting, with open fields and woodland all around. Croudace Homes seeks to enhance this connection with nature through careful landscaping and thoughtful blending of the homes with their locale, so that the development makes a positive contribution to life here.

Ochre Meadows is a place of real character that reflects its local environment and vernacular. Five distinct character areas create an attractive neighbourhood, with traditional features such as timber doors and casement windows, open eaves, red brick and natural slate roof tiling.

The homes have been designed to follow the principles of the Englefield Estate, to whom the land once belonged to, ensuring that they feel part of the local area and are rooted in their geographical context. Working together with Englefield Estate and ADAM Architecture, Croudace Homes is creating a place to be proud of now and for future generations, that will develop happily and healthily as the community evolves.

A central green acts as a natural heart for the community where residents can meet or children can play. A variety of planting, including a community orchard, provides a beautiful natural setting for the homes and creates interest throughout the seasons.
